directive: Implement mapper.ts as a stateless per-alert-rule function with signature mapRule(rule: AlertRule): PDEventPayload. Batching is a caller concern — p4 (backfill) will collect mapped payloads into arrays before calling the bulk endpoint; that is not mapper scope. All DSL fields must be represented losslessly; unknown fields go into custom_details. Unit tests cover each severity tier with one input/one output assertions.
rationale: This is a design_decision where bulk batching is being considered at the wrong abstraction layer. The mapper's responsibility is transformation (DSL rule → PD payload), not transport batching. A per-alert stateless function composes into bulk trivially via rules.map(mapRule), making it fully compatible with p4's backfill throughput requirement — the backfill caller batches already-mapped payloads before hitting the bulk endpoint. The inverse is not true: a bulk mapper cannot be cleanly decomposed for the existing single-alert dispatch path without introducing the same wrapper complexity being deferred. Per-alert also matches the existing dispatcher patterns the Executor cited, avoids forcing an unnecessary interface change on p3, and keeps unit tests simple (one input, one output per case). Non-goals are preserved: no DSL changes, no new service, no new secrets.
